docs.description: Simple bordered card primitives with floating border labels and semantic HTML support.

anatomy.name: Card
anatomy.note: Main bordered card surface with variant, size, and as props

anatomy.name: CardLabel
anatomy.note: Floating border label with variant='border' or variant='gap'

anatomy.name: CardHeader
anatomy.note: Header region with grid layout and bottom border

anatomy.name: CardTitle
anatomy.note: Title text with configurable heading level (as prop)

anatomy.name: CardAction
anatomy.note: Action slot in header (positioned top-right via grid)

notes.title: Variants
notes.content: Card is simple and bordered by default. Use variant='panel' to opt into an elevated panel-like treatment.

notes.title: Floating Border Labels
notes.content: Use CardLabel variant='border' for a boxed border label, or variant='gap' for a border cutout label with no border around the text. When using CardLabel, add pt-6 (or similar) to sibling CardContent to clear the floating label.

notes.title: Size
notes.content: Card supports size='sm' | 'md' | 'lg' for max-width constraints. Default is full width.

notes.title: Heading Level
notes.content: CardTitle renders an h3 by default. Use the as prop to change the heading level (h2, h3, h4, h5).

notes.title: Header Actions
notes.content: Use CardAction inside CardHeader to place an action (button, badge) in the top-right corner. The header switches to a two-column grid layout automatically when CardAction is present.

notes.title: Semantic HTML
notes.content: Card renders a div by default. Use as='article' for self-contained content, as='section' for grouped content, or as='aside' for tangential content.

notes.title: Accessible Article Cards
notes.content: When using as='article', connect the card to its heading with aria-labelledby so screen readers announce card boundaries. Give CardTitle an id and pass the same id to Card's aria-labelledby. See the Article example.
